title: Oskan blood eater

'''Oskan blood eaters''' were bipedal carnivores found on a variety of planets, often used as guards for penal colonies. They had two large hooves on each foot and four arms, each ending in a blade-like claw. They fed on any animal with [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygenated]] blood, preferring large mammalian creatures such as [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. The sight of a humanoid or other prey animal was enough to send them into a violent feeding frenzy. Though their senses were not particularly acute, they were determined trackers, who would hunt escaping prey for weeks.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

Blood eaters were notable for the gruesome way they killed and ate their prey. They would attack by slashing their prey to bits with their four large, slashing claws, leaving nothing more than a pile of flesh and blood. They would then lower their heads, and drink the remains through their perforated mouths.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

There was little variation between individual blood eaters, partly because they reproduced through a [[Wikipedia:Parthenogenesis|parthenogenetic]] process which made every blood eater almost genetically identical to its mother, and partly because of an efficient mutation correction mechanism in their cells. Despite this, they could thrive in a variety of environments on many worlds.<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

[[File:Oskan_blood_eater_2.jpg|thumb|left|300px|[[Nim Abek]]'s blood eater.]]

Although they were found in several different systems, blood eaters did not appear in the mythologies of any planet, as carnivores of their size would be expected to. This, together with their genetic peculiarities, led many to believe that they were artificially created creatures. [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence's]] [[Destabilization|Destab Branch]] supported these rumors, claiming that they were developed by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]]. In actuality, they were bio-engineered during the early [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] by the mad [[Geneticist/Legends|genetic]] [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] [[Zeta Magnus]].<ref name="Skyewalkers: A Clone Wars Story">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] would later experiment with the blood eater pups that were recovered from Zeta Magnus's [[Kharys' fortress|fortress]] laboratory by Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]].<ref name="Creatures of the Galaxy" />

Oskan blood eaters were popular with the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] underworld. The crimelord [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] once kept a blood eater as a "pet" in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|his palace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="The Hutt Gambit">''[[The Hutt Gambit]]''</ref> [[Chalmer Trillili]], leader of a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] gang called "[[The Scourge]]," also owned a blood eater, recovered from an Imperial vessel, which he kept as a guard animal in his hideout on [[Dega]].<ref name="Operation: Elrood">[[Operation: Elrood (book)|''Operation: Elrood'']]</ref> The [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] shipjacker [[Nim Abek]] also kept one for gladiatorial combat.<ref name="Secrets of the Sisar Run">''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]''</ref> [[Zeta Magnus]] grew blood eaters in his [[Kharys' fortress|palace]] on the planet [[Skye/Legends|Skye]] and unleashed a small pack of them in subterranean tunnels underneath it as guards. However, this pack was killed thanks to the efforts of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and the commando unit known as [[Tark Squad]].<ref name="Skyewalkers: A Clone Wars Story">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>
